ontology.foodex2_description: Samosa — deep-fried pastry with vegetable filling
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated veg samosa code; A0FKP (filled pastry / savoury snack) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept for samosa identified in published SNOMED CT release as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium veg samosa weighs approximately 60 g and is a typical single-serve snack portion as sold at Singapore hawker centres and Indian snack stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Samosa (Vegetable) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all per-100g fields. Values are accepted as-is. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for samosa (NDB 21052) as HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this item; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; samosa / filled wheat pastry analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for veg samosa found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: wheat-flour filled savoury pastry (GI ~50–60, median 55) per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Per the standard GI classification scale (low <55, medium 56–69, high ≥70), GI 55 falls in the low category. GL per serving = (55 × 33.16 × 0.60) / 100 = 10.9, rounded to 11.0 g for a 60 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 388 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (166 mg/100 g) and low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit veg samosa to one small piece occasionally, as the high sodium content (388 mg/100 g) combined with low potassium (166 mg/100 g) and low fibre provides no meaningful blood-pressure offset; the deep-fried fat load further elevates cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (33.2 g/100 g) from maida pastry and potato filling with low fibre (1.8 g/100 g) and estimated GI of 55 yielding GL of approximately 11 per piece
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit veg samosa because the refined wheat flour (maida) crust and potato filling deliver a low-to-medium-GI but high-glycaemic-load carbohydrate hit (GL ≈ 11 per piece) with minimal fibre buffering, leading to meaningful postprandial glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 17.5 g/100 g including saturated fat ~3.8 g/100 g and trans fat ~0.2 g/100 g from deep-frying; sodium 388 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise veg samosa consumption due to the combination of deep-frying-derived saturated and trans fats, elevated sodium, and low fibre, all of which adversely affect lipid profiles, blood pressure, and endothelial function.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs; trans fat present; potato (nightshade) may trigger solanine-related inflammation in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried samosa as the AGE burden from high-temperature frying and the presence of trans fat are pro-inflammatory; nightshade-sensitive patients should additionally avoid the potato filling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Veg Samosa is a popular deep-fried Indian savoury pastry widely consumed in Singapore as a hawker snack and street food. The outer shell is made from refined wheat flour (maida) that is kneaded into a stiff dough, shaped into a cone, filled with a spiced mixture of mashed potato and green peas, and then deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crisp. The result is a triangular pastry with a flaky, crunchy exterior and a soft, warmly spiced interior. Flavour notes include earthy potato, mild heat from green chilli and cumin, and a savoury depth from coriander and garam masala. It is typically served with tamarind chutney and green mint-coriander chutney. Nutritionally, veg samosa is energy-dense at 310 kcal/100 g, with significant fat (17.5 g) from deep-frying, moderate refined carbohydrate (33.2 g), and modest protein (5.1 g). Dietary fibre is low at 1.8 g/100 g and sodium is elevated at 388 mg/100 g. The refined flour crust and potato filling give an estimated glycaemic index of 55 (low by standard classification), with a glycaemic load of approximately 11 per standard 60 g piece. Clinically, veg samosa is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high refined carbohydrate load, elevated sodium, and deep-frying-derived saturated and trans fats. It is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-compatible but contains gluten.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a samosa?
Is samosa good for high blood pressure?
What is inside a veg samosa?
How much sodium in a samosa?
What is the GI of samosa?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 with exact match to veg samosa. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from USDA FoodData Central samosa entry (NDB 21052) and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 international tables.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.8 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (7.2 g/100 g), polyunsaturated fat (5.1 g/100 g), and trans fat (0.2 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 21052 (samosa, vegetable) as HPB Foodo does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this item. These values should be replaced with direct HPB measurements when available. 2. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific veg samosa preparation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central NDB 21052 at the level of individual vitamins and trace minerals as of 2026. 3. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for veg samosa was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026. Per the standard GI classification scale (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70), GI 55 is classified as low. 4. food_group corrected from input value 'Grains and staples / Baking ingredients' to 'Snacks / Fried pastry snacks' as veg samosa is a prepared snack dish, not a raw baking ingredient. 5. cholesterol_mg 14.0 is retained from HPB source; this is consistent with the small amount of egg or dairy sometimes used in dough, though many veg samosa recipes are egg-free — value accepted as per HPB source.

Updated gi_source notes and record notes to document this classification. Updated type2_diabetes guidance to reflect corrected GI category.
